title: REPLACE PARTITION silently fails when one of the partition keys is of type Bool
description:
### Company or project name
CloudQuery
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
When using `REPLACE PARTITION` on two tables that have a Bool-type column as part of the partition key, the operation succeeds but actually does not do a replacement. The target table's data stays the same as before.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
Tested with ClickHouse Server 25.6.13 and 25.10.2, both show this behavior.
### How to reproduce
Using 25.10.2.
### 1. Create tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE target_table
(
id UInt32,
country String,
active Bool
)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
PARTITION BY (country, active)
ORDER BY id;
CREATE TABLE source_table
(
id UInt32,
country String,
active Bool
)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
PARTITION BY (country, active)
ORDER BY id;
```
### 2. Insert initial data
Old data in `target_table`:
```sql
INSERT INTO target_table VALUES
(1, 'US', 1),
(2, 'US', 1),
(3, 'UK', 0);
```
New data in `source_table`:
```sql
INSERT INTO source_table VALUES
(10, 'US', 1),
(20, 'US', 1);
```
### 3a. Verify initial target contents
```
SELECT *, _partition_id
FROM target_table
ORDER BY id, country, active;
```
Should see:
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE target_table_2 (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, country VARCHAR(255), active BOOLEAN, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE source_table_2 (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, country VARCHAR(255), active BOOLEAN, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO target_table_2 (country, active, created_at) VALUES ('US', TRUE, now()), ('US', TRUE, now()), ('UK', FALSE, now());
INSERT INTO source_table_2 (country, active, created_at) VALUES ('US', TRUE, now()), ('US', TRUE, now()), ('UK', FALSE, now());
DELETE FROM target_table_2 WHERE (country, active) IN (SELECT country, active FROM source_table_2 WHERE country = 'US' AND active = TRUE);
key_bug_features: REPLACE PARTITION with Bool-type column fails silently
